payload.answer: The proposal to store an entire 8000-word transcript as a single conversation_summary card is noted. Durability requires that the memory card be created with the full text content and an explicit reference to provenance and timestamp. Ambiguities about indexing, searchability, and future updates should be resolved by creating structured sub-cards or metadata with versioning; however, for now the system should persist the single-card approach as a provisional memory representation, while acknowledging potential future granularity needs.

entity_resolution.reason: Transcript-level card aggregates many entities and relationships; per-preservation guidelines, entity resolution should occur only when identifying discrete, resolvable facts within the transcript. No concrete entities are extracted in this pass.

memory_cards.statement: An 8000-word transcript is proposed to be stored as a single conversation_summary card.

details: Received instruction to return a single JSON object matching the schema.
Acknowledged requirement to preserve ambiguity and avoid presenting superseded facts as current.

repair_options: Create a single conversation_summary card containing the entire 8000-word transcript with metadata (source, timestamp, author).
Alternatively, partition the transcript into smaller logical segments (e.g., per speaker or topic) and store as multiple cards linked to a parent conversation_summary card.
Add explicit versioning and a retention policy to avoid superseding outdated summaries.
Record a note indicating potential retrieval limitations due to single-card size and plan for indexing improvements.
